combined_summary: The three most critical actions are: (1) securing a fixed quarterly clay shipment with real-time tracking by March 31, 2026, to prevent supply disruptions; (2) purchasing $500,000 liability insurance for volunteers and enforcing mandatory safety training by April 15, 2026, to mitigate legal and operational risks; and (3) installing IoT environmental sensors and enforcing a thermal curfew by July 1, 2026, to ensure safe, energy-efficient operations during winter peaks. These actions directly address the highest-risk gaps identified: supply chain volatility, volunteer liability, and winter energy demand—each of which could derail Year 1 viability if unaddressed.

feedback.title: Secure Quarterly Clay Shipments
feedback.description:
- Finalize a fixed quarterly contract with a single Danish supplier (e.g., Køge Keramik) by 2026-Mar-31 at 14:00, specifying consolidated container shipments of 500 kg clay per quarter.
- Require the supplier to provide real-time tracking via API integration with a logistics dashboard by 2026-Apr-15 at 10:00.
- Confirm customs clearance protocols in Greenlandic language (Kalaallisut) and Danish for all documentation by 2026-Apr-20 at 12:00.

feedback.title: Install Passive Solar Design Elements
feedback.description:
- Commission a thermal modeling study using EnergyPlus software by 2026-May-05 at 16:00, simulating winter conditions at 90% occupancy (12–16 people).
- Install south-facing double-glazed windows (minimum 2.5 m² each, total 10 m²) and thermal mass walls (30 cm concrete or rammed earth) by 2026-Jun-30 at 18:00.
- Conduct pre-fit-out thermal audit with a certified Arctic building specialist by 2026-Jul-10 at 14:00.

feedback.title: Implement Rotating Instructor Schedule
feedback.description:
- Develop a digital shift calendar with automated alerts by 2026-May-10 at 11:00, assigning two instructors per week across four 4-week cycles.
- Mandate cross-training in hand-building, wheel-throwing, and kiln operation for all four instructors by 2026-May-25 at 15:00.
- Conduct first joint session rehearsal with full rotation coverage by 2026-Jun-15 at 13:00.

feedback.title: Establish On-Site Material Buffer
feedback.description:
- Procure and store 50 kg of clay (10% of annual volume) as a buffer stock in sealed, climate-controlled storage by 2026-May-20 at 10:00.
- Pre-negotiate air freight contract with Nordic Cargo for emergency delivery (max 72-hour transit) by 2026-May-31 at 14:00.
- Appoint a local logistics coordinator to monitor Arctic shipping alerts via Danish Maritime Authority feed by 2026-Jun-01 at 09:00.

feedback.title: Purchase Liability Insurance for Volunteers
feedback.description:
- Acquire a $500,000 general liability policy covering volunteer-led sessions by 2026-Mar-31 at 14:00.
- Require all retired artists joining the volunteer pool to sign a liability waiver and complete 4-hour safety training (fire, dust, equipment) by 2026-Apr-10 at 12:00.
- Allocate 100,000 DKK from contingency fund to cover insurance premium and training materials by 2026-Apr-15 at 10:00.

feedback.title: Deploy Real-Time Environmental Monitoring
feedback.description:
- Install IoT sensors (temperature, humidity, CO₂) in drying zone and kiln area by 2026-Jun-20 at 16:00.
- Integrate sensor data into a public dashboard accessible to staff and instructors by 2026-Jul-01 at 10:00.
- Enforce 'thermal curfew' protocol: 2-hour kiln sessions followed by 30-minute cooldowns during peak winter use by 2026-Aug-01 at 14:00.

feedback.title: Conduct Mandatory Safety Training
feedback.description:
- Deliver mandatory safety training for all users (students, instructors, volunteers) by 2026-Jul-15 at 14:00, covering HEPA ventilation, fire extinguisher use, emergency shutdown, and silica exposure.
- Post emergency procedures at every station with bilingual (Danish/Greenlandic) signage by 2026-Jul-25 at 12:00.
- Conduct monthly safety drills with full participation by 2026-Aug-01 at 10:00.

feedback.title: Verify Cultural Consent Protocols
feedback.description:
- Form an advisory council with at least three elders and two local artists by 2026-Mar-31 at 14:00.
- Obtain written consent for all traditional motifs (e.g., qajaq-inspired forms) used in courses and exhibitions by 2026-Apr-20 at 12:00.
- Submit all exhibition content and curriculum for community review prior to launch by 2026-Jul-30 at 16:00.
